__fixture__.name: shell_crash_lost
__fixture__.scenario: Desktop shell process exits on an unhandled panic while the user was typing. The shell has restart_class = never_auto_restart; the supervisor does NOT silently respawn it. The forensic packet carries the exit reason, the revoked ticket set (all shell-held subscriptions, capability envelopes, and extension permission manifests), the last-good checkpoint on dirty buffers and local history, and projects escalation_state_after = lost so the next launch starts from warming. No exit_reason_class is retried inside a short window because the shell is never auto-restarted.

heartbeat_snapshot.note: Shell does not heartbeat to the supervisor on a child lifecycle line; the supervisor observes shell exit via desktop-session wait.

resource_snapshot.note: Background CPU / IO were nominal; crash was a localized panic in the command-routing path.

capability_envelope_snapshot.note: Shell held editor, vfs, and search subscription envelopes plus two extension permission manifests at the moment of crash.

last_good_checkpoint.note: Dirty buffers and local history checkpoints were persisted 4 seconds before the crash; the user's in-flight typing is recoverable on next launch.

degraded_state_lineage.note: Shell exited; supervisor projects lost. Recovery owner is the user via relaunch.

revoked_tickets.note: Editor buffer subscription revoked on shell loss; consumers will see resync_required with host_restart on next launch.
Extension permission manifest revoked; re-admission required on next launch.
Target capability envelope invalidated; re-issued on next launch under policy admission.

emergency_action_linkage.note: No emergency-action record linked to this crash; cause is a localized unhandled panic.

workspace_scope.note: Shell was not bound to a resolved execution context at the moment of crash.

summary: Desktop shell crashed on an unhandled panic; supervisor projects lost, revokes all shell-held subscriptions, capability envelopes, and extension permission manifests, and preserves the last-good local-history checkpoint for the next launch.
